primary_source: (Wildberger, 2003) A Combinatorial Construction for Simply-Laced Lie Algebras, Advances in Applied Mathematics 30:385-396; (Wildberger, 2020) The Mutation Game, Coxeter-Dynkin Graphs, and Generalized Root Systems, Algebra Colloquium 27.
source_claim: Initial Tier-1 triage reading (pre-correction) claimed E_8 is excluded from Wildberger's integer simply-laced Lie algebra constructions per Theorem 2.1 of (Wildberger, 2003), which requires a two-neighbourly maximal X-heap and lists only A_n, D_n, E_6, E_7.
qa_translation: Test whether the (Wildberger, 2003) minuscule-poset Lie algebra construction extends to E_8 via QA integer populations on a Coxeter-Dynkin graph.
failure_mode: Initial theory doc docs/theory/QA_WILDBERGER_E8_RECONCILIATION.md (pre-correction) concluded E_8 was excluded from Wildberger integer constructions. Claim stood until 2026-04-14 post-commit primary-source find: the (Wildberger, 2020) Mutation Game paper (Algebra Colloquium 27, 79pp) extends the 2003 framework via mutations on bidirected multigraphs and INCLUDES E_8 per Theorem 0.1/0.3 and the 120-element R+(E_8,7) poset of integer-coefficient vectors. Initial reading missed the later paper — a retrieval gap, not a mathematical obstruction. Correction landed as cert [244] QA_MUTATION_GAME_ROOT_LATTICE_CERT.v1 using the 2020 construction with 240-root orbit, Cartan determinant 1, and Weyl braid witnesses.
source_attribution: Will Dale + Claude 2026-04-14; corrected by discovery of the (Wildberger, 2020) Mutation Game paper; superseded by cert [244] which uses the 2020 construction
